taxonomy: Quercus obconicus belongs to the family Fagaceae, commonly known as the beech family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics, including its leaf shape and the structure of its reproductive organs. The genus Quercus, to which Quercus obconicus belongs, is a group of deciduous trees native to the Northern Hemisphere. Quercus obconicus is also classified as a member of the order Fagales, a group of flowering plants that includes other beech and oak species. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics of Quercus obconicus with other plants in this family and order.
uses: Quercus obconicus, also known as the scrub live oak, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits.
